title: semrel configuration
description: Configuration schema for semrel — semantic release automation. https://semrel.io

schemaVersion.description: Schema version of this config file. Use `semrel migrate` to upgrade. Currently 1.

branches.description: List of release branches. semrel only creates releases when run on one of these branches.

tagPrefix.description: Prefix prepended to the version number when creating git tags. Default: "v".

rules.description: Mapping of conventional commit types to semver bump levels. Overrides the built-in defaults.

plugins.description: Plugins to run during the release pipeline (providers, hooks, updaters, generators, conditions).

version_ceiling.description: Maximum version semrel will release. Semver string, e.g. "1.0.0". Releases above this ceiling are handled by ceiling_strategy.

ceiling_strategy.description: What to do when the computed next version exceeds version_ceiling. Default: "clamp".

commit_changelog.description: Whether semrel commits CHANGELOG.md back to the repo before creating the release tag. Default: true.

tag_exists_strategy.description: What to do when the computed tag already exists. Default: "update-changelog".

BranchConfig.description: Configuration for a single release branch.

prerelease.description: Pre-release identifier appended to the version (e.g. "beta", "alpha.1"). Omit for stable releases.

maintenance.description: Mark as a maintenance branch — only patch bumps are allowed. Auto-detected for N.x / N.M.x patterns.

ReleaseRule.description: Maps a conventional commit type to a semver bump level.

bump.description: Semver bump level triggered by this commit type.

PluginConfig.description: Configuration for a single semrel plugin.

uses.description: Official plugin name as published in the semrel registry (e.g. "provider-github").

name.description: Local alias for this plugin instance. Useful when the same plugin is used multiple times.

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the plugin binary. Use instead of 'uses' for local/custom plugins.

args.description: Plugin-specific arguments passed as key-value pairs. Refer to the plugin's documentation.

phase.description: Pipeline phase in which this plugin runs. Default: "release".
